message.content:
You are an adversarial refuter. ASSUME this finding is FALSE and try to prove it from the actual code at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay. Find the code path/contract/test that makes the PoC impossible or moot. Only survives=true if you genuinely cannot disprove it.

O-4: Provenance-trail artifact tempfile is leaked on every tracked run (delete=False, never unlinked)
File: src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:739
Description: On successful completion run_study persists the provenance trail to a NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False) and logs it as an MLflow artifact, but the file is never removed afterward. MLflow copies the file into the artifact store, so the original temp file remains in the system temp dir indefinitely. Every successful run that has a tracker leaks one *_provenance.json temp file; over a long-lived node (this is the production compute node) these accumulate without bound. The sibling persist_trail() path is caller-controlled, but this internal artifact path has no cleanup.
PoC: Run run_study(plan, gate_handler=auto_approve, tracker=MlflowExperimentTracker()) N times; ls $TMPDIR/*_provenance.json shows N leaked files. The tempfile is created at pipeline.py:739 with delete=False and there is no os.unlink in the _log_trail closure or the surrounding finally.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.

SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.text: The code at line 739-744 confirms the structure. The `_log_trail` closure creates a `NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False)`, logs it as an artifact via `t.log_artifact(rid, path)`, and indeed there is no `os.unlink`. Let me check the `track` helper and `log_artifact` to see if cleanup happens elsewhere.

content.text:
Confirmed. `MlflowExperimentTracker.log_artifact` just delegates to MLflow's `client.log_artifact`, which copies the file into the artifact store and does NOT delete the source. There is no unlink anywhere in the path.

Let me check whether there's any test asserting cleanup, or any wrapper/finally that might remove temp files, to make sure I'm not missing a cleanup path.

content.text:
The test asserts the artifact is logged but says nothing about temp-file cleanup. No test contradicts the finding.

I've fully traced the path:
- `pipeline.py:739` creates `NamedTemporaryFile(delete=False, suffix="_provenance.json")`
- The `with` block only closes the file handle; `delete=False` means closing does NOT remove the file
- `t.log_artifact(rid, path)` → `MlflowExperimentTracker.log_artifact` → `client.log_artifact` (MLflow copies, never removes source)
- No `os.unlink`/`os.remove` anywhere in the closure, the `track` helper, or the surrounding `finally` block
- The only `unlink` in the codebase is in `versioning.py:81`, an unrelated path

The finding stands.

input.reasoning: Confirmed against the actual code; could not disprove. At /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/pipeline.py:733-746, the _log_trail closure creates tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile("w", suffix="_provenance.json", delete=False, ...). The with-block only closes the handle; with delete=False, closing does NOT unlink the file. It then calls t.log_artifact(rid, path). MlflowExperimentTracker.log_artifact (src/assay_engine/observability/tracking.py:92-93) merely delegates to MLflow's client.log_artifact, which copies the file into the artifact store and leaves the source temp file in place. The track() helper (pipeline.py:317-324) only invokes fn and traps exceptions — no cleanup. The surrounding finally block (pipeline.py:771-778) only closes the trace ctx and calls tracker.end_run; it never unlinks the temp file. A repo-wide grep for unlink/os.remove shows the only such call is in persistence/versioning.py:81 (an unrelated atomic-replace path), not in the provenance-artifact path. No test asserts cleanup (test_pipeline.py:451-454 only asserts the artifact was logged). Therefore every successful run with a tracker leaks one *_provenance.json file in the system temp dir, unbounded over a long-lived node. The PoC is accurate. Minor caveat: actual unboundedness on a real node depends on OS tmp-reaper policy (e.g., systemd-tmpfiles / macOS periodic cleanup may eventually remove old files), but the code itself performs no cleanup, so the defect as described is real.

content.text: I've completed the adversarial review of finding O-4 and could not disprove it. The leak is real: the provenance temp file at `pipeline.py:739` is created with `delete=False` and never unlinked anywhere in the closure, the `track` helper, or the surrounding `finally` block. `MlflowExperimentTracker.log_artifact` only copies the file into the artifact store. The finding survives.
